sutta:
5 5 monks these 5 things lead to the confounding the disappearance of true dhamma sadhama what 5 here in mounts the monks master not dhamma this monks is the first thing that leads to the confounding the disappearance of sat dhamma secondly they teach not others dhamma in detail as heard as learned this monks is the second thing thirdly they make not others speak it in detail as heard as learned this month is the third thing they make no repetition of it in detail as heard as learned this monks is the fourth thing again monks the monks do not in their hearts turn over and ponder upon dhamma they review it not in their minds this monks is the fifth thing that leads to the confounding the disappearance of saddam these are the 5 things that lead to the confounding the disappearance of saddam so these are the conditions that cause the disappearance of the true dharma earlier i think you remember i mentioned another suitable so in the chapter of the force the conditions that caused the disappearance of the true dharma 1 is that the monks are stubborn they don't don't want to train in the training and other conditions that suit our sangutra nikaya 4.160 this is another suta there's another 1 also in the song yutani kaya 16.13 so this 1 this the first condition is that the monks do not master dhamma they don't take the trouble to study the sutas because in the anguttanikaya 4.180 the buddha said dhamma refers to the suttas the original discourses of the buddha and the 4 nikayas so if a monk does not master the sutas he he will find it hard to differentiate between the true dhamma and the false dhamma because there's such a thing as false dharma mentioned in the sanghita nikaya 16.13 so the first thing is important for a manga to master the sutas the second 1 they do not teach others the sutas the dhamma in detail that's the second 1 they do not teach the sutras the third 1 they do not make others speak it in detail that means their disciples because in the days of the buddha they didn't have books so to prolong the life of the dharma they had to recite it and carry it on from generation to generation so that they had to train the younger monks to speak it and to recite it to memorize it so this is the third condition if they do not make others speak it in detail that means memorize it the fourth 1 they make no repetition of it in detail as heard as learned this 1 is the monks themselves the senior monks and they do not repeat it this is by repeating it now that they uh memorize it now and also they understand it better if you remember this 1 of the ways that a person attains arya then the fifth 1 do not they do not reflect and ponder over the dhamma that they have learned this is the fifth thing that leads to the disappearance because by reflecting and pondering over dhamma we come to understand it better yeah so for this another condition for the attaining of aryah you remember 1 of the earliest uttars mentioned the the 5 ways of attaining aryah is 1 in is learning the dhamma the second 1 is teaching the dhamma third 1 is repeating the dhamma for 1 is reflecting upon dhamma the fifth 1 is during meditation uh these are the 5 times so here here this is um these are the conditions now if you want to prolong the dhamma the first 1 is applicable to us now now we should master the dhamma after the sutas because if we take no interest in the sutas it will cause the sutas to disappear that was already worn by the buddha then we should teach the dhamma also as we know the dhamma so we should teach it to others the third 1 is not quite applicable now to make others speak it in detail because we have it all written down in books the fourth 1 repeating it is uh if we have the interest in the inclination is good to repeat the sutras as we know them the fifth 1 is to ponder over the sutras this 1 is this is quite important whatever sutas whatever dharma we hear we should reflect on it and try to understand it better
